attacking_style: Launches long-range attacks on final climbs (1-3km finishing ascents), exploiting gradient to drop pure sprinters while overpowering climbers in reduced sprints. Increasingly attacks from distance rather than contesting traditional leadout sprints.
team_tactics: Team executes controlled attrition via hard pacing on categorized climbs 20-40km from finish to thin sprint field. Matthews often works himself (intermediate sprints, crosswind chases) before still contesting finale—unusual diesel capacity for sprinter profile.
terrain_preference: Thrives on 2-4km uphill finishes with 3-6% gradient and selective categorized climbs preceding sprint. Excels in disorganized reduced bunch sprints. Struggles on pan-flat pure sprints and steep 10%+ kickers where pure climbers accelerate.
vulnerability: Lacks final 50m acceleration on steep gradients above 10%. Poor heat tolerance documented. Cannot survive sustained mountain stages. Positioning perfect but conversion rate low in traditional flat sprints—hesitant finishing rather than power deficit.
key_patterns: Systematically hunts intermediate sprint bonus seconds for GC/jersey strategies, 6+ observations across tours
Wins via team-executed climb attrition 20-40km out, not traditional leadout trains, 4+ examples
Repeats victories on identical finish profiles years apart, suggesting specific reconnaissance preparation
Shows late-Tour form progression pattern, closing points gaps in final week, 3+ observations
Attacks over climbs despite sprinter classification, forcing climbers to respond, 5+ instances
Exceptional diesel engine: works for team then still contests finale, documented 4+ times
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Giro win after 8-year drought shows climbing form improvement. Team executing attrition tactics confidently again. Bonus second hunting strategy demonstrates tactical intelligence maturation despite mechanical setback at Romandie.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 2
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 6
